blockingIssues.description: Reply contention tests in test/thread.test.ts (line ~1117) and integration overlapping tests in test/integration.test.ts (line ~1206) use synchronous looped calls rather than true overlapping/parallel contention, not exercising real lock contention as the feature intends.

The newly added INSERT-conflict recovery branches in src/message.ts (sendMessage catch ~line 233 and replyMessage catch ~line 718) are never exercised by tests because the pre-INSERT SELECT dedupe check finds the canonical row first, leaving the race-recovery code path unverified.

appliedUpdates.description: Added concurrency testing guidance to architecture.md: better-sqlite3 is synchronous single-connection, so true INSERT-level race contention cannot be exercised with single-handle in-process tests. Multi-process or multi-handle patterns are needed.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update mors-worker skill to explicitly reference .factory/library/user-testing.md for stable CLI invocation patterns (MORS_CONFIG_DIR + node dist/index.js) during manual verification steps, so workers don't waste cycles discovering the pattern through trial and error.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Config-dir-permissions worker (c24c6152) transcript shows repeated failed attempts using 'npx tsx src/cli.ts init --json' before falling back to programmatic checks. The stable pattern is already documented in .factory/library/user-testing.md but not cross-referenced from the skill.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that workers must ensure newly added catch/recovery branches are actually exercised by tests (branch coverage for error-handling paths). When better-sqlite3's synchronous nature prevents true race conditions, workers should use targeted fault injection or mocking to verify recovery branches rather than claiming concurrent coverage from sequential loops.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Dedupe-concurrency worker added INSERT-conflict recovery code that is structurally unreachable in single-handle synchronous tests, then reported tests as covering the feature. The skill's TDD procedure was reported as followed despite tests not going red-then-green for the recovery branch.

rejectedObservations.observation: TDD procedure fidelity concern: tests passed before implementation and skillFeedback.followedProcedure was set to true
rejectedObservations.reason: This is already captured as a systemic issue in suggestedGuidanceUpdates (branch coverage enforcement). The procedure-fidelity concern is a symptom of the same root cause — unclear expectations for recovery-branch testing — not a separate actionable item.
